name: Ryo Kawasaki
abstract: Ryo Kawasaki (川崎 燎, Kawasaki Ryō; February 25, 1947 – April 13, 2020) was a Japanese jazz fusion guitarist, composer and band leader, best known as one of the first musicians to develop and popularise the fusion genre and for helping to develop the guitar synthesizer in collaboration with Roland Corporation and Korg. His album Ryo Kawasa…

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] This is a funny but interesting band: Randy Brecker, Barry Rogers, Dave Taylor, Tom Malone, you, Sato, Ryo Kawasaki, Francisco Santino on bass, and Harvey Mason on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Elvin Jones
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...At that time you had Andrew White, Ari Brown, Ryo? Was he playing guitar? [INTERVIEWEE] Ryo Kawasaki? No. It was Marvin Horn, I think he was playing guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: Elvin Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Pat LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: It was me, David Williams, who was from Trinidad; and Elvin; and Ryo Kawasaki was from Japan.
